declared.title: Tanzania: Surge in online LGBTIQ censorship and other targeted blocks
declared.selection_rationale: Full-length OONI blog report (Maria Xynou, Mehul Gulati, Tori Fransis), not a short Finding -- Key Findings, Introduction, Methods (with an explicit Limitations subsection), Findings (5 subsections), Conclusion, Acknowledgements. Genuine coverage contribution: this corpus's existing Tanzania entries cover platform blocking (X/Twitter) and human-rights context generally, but nothing on LGBTIQ-specific censorship -- this report documents the blocking of Grindr, Romeo, Hornet (LGBTIQ social networks), ILGA/OutRight International (LGBTIQ rights orgs), Queerty (LGBTIQ news), and The Trevor Project (LGBTIQ suicide prevention), plus separately Tinder/OKCupid (dating), Change.org/Global Fund for Women (human-rights grants/petitions), Clubhouse/4chan, and ProtonVPN -- all via consistent TLS-interference (ClientHello timeout) signatures across multiple networks, analyzed over a 13-month period (Jan 2023-Jan 2024). Grounds the technical finding in real human-rights context: Tanzania's colonial-era Penal Code criminalizing same-sex acts, a 2018 government task-force targeting suspected gay people (UN High Commissioner response cited), and the April 2023 murder of Milembe Suleiman in a suspected homophobic attack. Publication date confirmed exactly from the post's own byline ("2024-04-11").
